description: ip address literal parsing and equality

tests.name: ipv4_eq_same
tests.description: two identical IPv4 literals should be equal
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("1.2.3.4") == ip("1.2.3.4") };

tests.name: ipv4_eq_different
tests.description: two different IPv4 literals should not be equal
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("1.2.3.4") == ip("5.6.7.8") };

tests.name: ipv4_ne
tests.description: != comparison on different IPv4 addresses
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("1.2.3.4") != ip("5.6.7.8") };

tests.name: ipv4_cidr_eq_same
tests.description: two identical IPv4 CIDR should be equal
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("10.0.0.0/8") == ip("10.0.0.0/8") };

tests.name: ipv4_cidr_eq_different_prefix
tests.description: same address but different prefix length should not be equal
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("10.0.0.0/8") == ip("10.0.0.0/16") };

tests.name: ipv6_eq_same
tests.description: two identical IPv6 literals should be equal
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("::1") == ip("::1") };

tests.name: ipv6_eq_different
tests.description: two different IPv6 literals should not be equal
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("::1") == ip("::2") };

tests.name: ipv6_cidr_eq_same
tests.description: two identical IPv6 CIDR should be equal
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("fe80::/10") == ip("fe80::/10") };

tests.name: ipv4_context_attr_eq
tests.description: ip literal equals context IP attribute
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("10.0.0.1") == context.ip };

tests.name: ipv4_context_attr_ne
tests.description: ip literal does not equal different context IP attribute
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("10.0.0.1") == context.ip };

tests.name: ipv4_full_octets
tests.description: IPv4 with all octets at boundary values
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("0.0.0.0") == ip("0.0.0.0") };

tests.name: ipv4_max_octets
tests.description: IPv4 with max octet values
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("255.255.255.255") == ip("255.255.255.255") };

tests.name: ipv6_full_form
tests.description: IPv6 full form with leading zeros equality

tests.name: ip_invalid_string
tests.description: invalid IP string should produce error and deny
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("not-an-ip") == ip("1.2.3.4") };

tests.name: ip_in_forbid
tests.description: forbid with IP comparison overrides permit
tests.policy:
permit (principal, action, resource);
forbid (principal, action, resource) when { ip("10.0.0.1") == context.ip };

tests.name: ipv4_prefix_zero
tests.description: IPv4 with /0 prefix should be valid
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("0.0.0.0/0") == ip("0.0.0.0/0") };

tests.name: ipv6_prefix_zero
tests.description: IPv6 with /0 prefix should be valid
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("::/0") == ip("::/0") };

tests.name: ipv6_trailing_double_colon
tests.description: IPv6 with trailing :: should be valid
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("2001:db8::") == ip("2001:db8::") };

tests.name: ipv4_vs_ipv6_cross_family
tests.description: IPv4 and IPv6 are different types and should not be equal
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("1.2.3.4") == ip("::1") };

tests.name: ipv4_leading_zeros_rejected
tests.description: IPv4 with leading zeros should be rejected (deny via error)
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("010.0.0.1") == ip("10.0.0.1") };

tests.name: ipv4_prefix_leading_zeros_rejected
tests.description: IPv4 CIDR with leading zeros in prefix should be rejected
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("10.0.0.0/08") == ip("10.0.0.0/8") };

tests.name: ipv6_five_digit_group_rejected
tests.description: IPv6 group with 5+ hex digits should be rejected
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("00001::1") == ip("1::1") };

tests.name: ipv6_prefix_leading_zeros_rejected
tests.description: IPv6 CIDR with leading zeros in prefix should be rejected
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("fe80::/064") == ip("fe80::/64") };

tests.name: ipv4_mapped_dot_notation_rejected
tests.description: IPv4-mapped IPv6 with dot notation should be rejected
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("::ffff:192.168.1.1") == ip("::ffff:192.168.1.1") };

tests.name: ipv4_mapped_hex_form_valid
tests.description: IPv4-mapped IPv6 in pure hex form should be valid
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("::ffff:c0a8:0101") == ip("::ffff:c0a8:0101") };

tests.name: ipv4_mapped_hex_ne_plain_ipv4
tests.description: IPv4-mapped hex and plain IPv4 are different address families
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("::ffff:c0a8:0101") == ip("192.168.1.1") };

tests.name: ip_empty_string_rejected
tests.description: empty string should be rejected as IP address
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("") == ip("1.2.3.4") };

tests.name: context_has_ip_keyword
tests.description: has operator should accept ip as unquoted attribute name
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { context has ip && context.ip == ip("10.0.0.1") };

tests.name: context_has_ip_keyword_missing
tests.description: has ip on context without ip attribute should be false
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { context has ip };

tests.name: ipv4_prefix_33_rejected
tests.description: IPv4 CIDR with prefix > 32 should be rejected
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("1.2.3.4/33") == ip("1.2.3.4/33") };

tests.name: ipv6_prefix_129_rejected
tests.description: IPv6 CIDR with prefix > 128 should be rejected
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("::1/129") == ip("::1/129") };

tests.name: ipv6_nine_groups_rejected
tests.description: IPv6 with 9 groups should be rejected
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("1:2:3:4:5:6:7:8:9") == ip("::1") };

tests.name: ipv6_triple_colon_rejected
tests.description: IPv6 with triple colon should be rejected
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("1:::2") == ip("::1") };

tests.name: ipv4_octet_256_rejected
tests.description: IPv4 with octet > 255 should be rejected
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("256.0.0.1") == ip("1.2.3.4") };

tests.name: ipv4_prefix_slash_only_rejected
tests.description: IPv4 with slash but no prefix digits should be rejected
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("1.2.3.4/") == ip("1.2.3.4") };

tests.name: ipv4_prefix_non_digit_rejected
tests.description: IPv4 CIDR with non-digit prefix should be rejected
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("1.2.3.4/abc") == ip("1.2.3.4") };

tests.name: ipv6_mixed_case_equal
tests.description: IPv6 uppercase and lowercase hex should be equal
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("2001:DB8::1") == ip("2001:db8::1") };

tests.name: ipv6_redundant_double_colon_rejected
tests.description: IPv6 with :: expanding to zero groups should be rejected
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("1:2:3:4::5:6:7:8") == ip("1:2:3:4:5:6:7:8") };

tests.name: ipv6_middle_double_colon
tests.description: IPv6 with :: in the middle should expand correctly
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("1:2::3:4") == ip("1:2::3:4") };

tests.name: ipv6_middle_double_colon_expanded
tests.description: IPv6 with :: in the middle equals full form

tests.name: ipv6_double_gap_rejected
tests.description: IPv6 with two :: gaps should be rejected
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("::1::2") == ip("::1") };

tests.name: ipv6_trailing_single_colon_rejected
tests.description: IPv6 with trailing single colon should be rejected
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("1:2:3:4:5:6:7:8:") == ip("1:2:3:4:5:6:7:8") };

tests.name: ipv6_leading_single_colon_rejected
tests.description: IPv6 with leading single colon should be rejected
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip(":1") == ip("::1") };

tests.name: principal_attr_ip_eq
tests.description: IP literal equals principal IP attribute
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("192.168.1.100") == principal.src_ip };

tests.name: principal_attr_ip_ne
tests.description: IP literal does not equal different principal IP attribute
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("192.168.1.100") == principal.src_ip };

tests.name: action_attr_ip_eq
tests.description: IP literal equals action IP attribute
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("10.0.0.1") == action.server_ip };

tests.name: resource_attr_ip_eq
tests.description: IP literal equals resource IP attribute
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("172.16.0.1") == resource.bind_ip };

tests.name: resource_attr_ip_ne
tests.description: IP literal does not equal different resource IP attribute
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("172.16.0.1") == resource.bind_ip };

tests.name: ipv4_octet_overflow_rejected
tests.description: IPv4 with extremely long octet should be rejected (overflow guard)
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("99999999999999999999.0.0.1") == ip("1.2.3.4") };

tests.name: ipv4_prefix_overflow_rejected
tests.description: IPv4 CIDR with extremely long prefix should be rejected (overflow guard)

tests.name: ipv6_prefix_overflow_rejected
tests.description: IPv6 CIDR with extremely long prefix should be rejected (overflow guard)
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("::1/99999999999999999999") == ip("::1/64") };

tests.name: ipv6_leading_zeros_group_valid
tests.description: IPv6 group with leading zeros is valid per Cedar spec
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("0001::1") == ip("1::1") };

tests.name: ipv6_leading_zero_hex_group_valid
tests.description: IPv6 group with leading zero in hex is valid per Cedar spec
tests.policy: permit (principal, action, resource) when { ip("01ab::1") == ip("1ab::1") };
